text: In software development, Git branching is an essential part of version control. Developers create feature branches to work on new features or bug fixes without affecting the main codebase. Each branch allows for independent changes. Once the work is complete, developers merge the feature branch back into the main branch. This is often done through a pull request, where team members can review the code before merging. This workflow helps maintain a clean project history and prevents conflicts. Overall, Git branching supports collaboration, keeps the main branch stable, and makes it easier to manage different versions of the code.
